title: Combining Separate Entities
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Combining Separate Entities Another way to improve performance is to combine several entities into a single entity. For example, the original model has separate entities for ORDERLINE, COVERED_ITEM, DELIVERYLINE, and PART. You can combine these into a single entity called ASSEMBLY as shown in the following illustration: Figure 1. Access Profile for Redefined Entity The access profile and the frames-within-frames diagram for this situation is shown in the following illustration: Figure 2. Access Profile and Frame-Within-Frames Using Redefined Entity The amended access profile is shown in the following table. Table 1. Access Profile Statistics for Redefined Entity Access profile Via Selected Occurrences (avg.)
